log.title: Log meal
log.hint: Live camera, gallery, or snapshot → AI estimates calories → edit → earn points
log.cameraDenied: Camera access denied. Allow camera in Telegram or pick a photo from gallery.
log.portionAdjustHint: Change grams to rescale macros proportionally
log.aiFallbackNoKey: AI unavailable: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY (using 450 kcal template).
log.aiFallbackApiError: AI unavailable — check API keys on Render (ANTHROPIC / OPENAI / GEMINI).
log.aiFallbackParseError: AI could not read this photo — try another shot or enter values manually.
log.error_NOT_FOOD: This doesn't look like food. Try another photo.
log.error_DUPLICATE_PHOTO: You already logged this photo recently.
log.error_ANALYZE_LIMIT: Daily AI scan limit reached. Upgrade to Pro or enter manually.
log.error_VOICE_ANALYZE_LIMIT: Free limit: 5 voice estimates per day. Pro is unlimited, or type manually.
log.voicePrompt: e.g. oatmeal with banana and coffee
log.voiceHint: Free: 5 voice estimates per day. Pro — unlimited. You can type instead.
log.voiceRecording: Recording — say what you ate, then tap Stop.
log.voiceMicDenied: Microphone access denied. Allow mic in Telegram or type your meal.
log.voiceAudioTooShort: Recording too short. Speak for 1–2 seconds, then stop.
log.voiceTranscribeNoKey: Speech transcription unavailable: set OPENAI_API_KEY (Whisper) or GEMINI_API_KEY.
log.voiceTranscribeFailed: Could not transcribe audio. Try again or type manually.
log.voiceUnsupported: Voice is not supported in this browser — type your meal instead.
log.voiceError: Could not recognize speech. Try again or type manually.
log.sourceVoice: Voice AI estimate {{confidence}}% — adjust if needed.
log.sourceBarcodeAi: AI barcode estimate {{confidence}}% — verify name and grams.
log.sourcePhotoOnly: Photo attached — enter macros manually (no AI).
log.sourceCatalog: From USDA product database — adjust portion if needed.
log.sourceBarcode: From barcode lookup — adjust grams if needed.
log.sourceBarcodeRu: From NutriCrew Russian catalog (200+ RU products) — adjust grams if needed.
log.sourceBarcodeOffRu: From Open Food Facts (Russia) — adjust grams if needed.
log.sourceBarcodeOff: From Open Food Facts — adjust grams if needed.
log.quickEntryHint: Barcode (200+ RU catalog + Open Food Facts) or USDA food search.
log.barcodeManualHint: Point the camera at a barcode or type digits from the package.
log.barcodeNotFound: Barcode not found in databases. You can estimate with AI below.
log.barcodeLookupError: Could not load product data. Check your connection.
log.barcodeAiHint: AI will guess the product from the code. Add the package name for better accuracy.
log.barcodeAiError: AI could not estimate this product. Enter macros manually.
log.catalogHint: Type at least 2 characters to search USDA-based foods.
log.offlineSaved: Saved offline — will sync when you submit

diary.recOnTrack: You're close to your {{goal}} target today — nice work.

settings.fontSizePreviewBody: This is how text will look in the app: cards, menu, and hints.
settings.hapticsHint: Vibration in the game and key actions
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Export your data or manage your account (GDPR).
settings.exportFailed: Could not export your data. Try again later.
settings.exportServerBusy: Server is waking up — wait a moment and try again.
settings.deleteConfirm: Delete all your NutriCrew data permanently?

privacySections: NutriCrew processes Telegram profile data, meal logs, photos, and team activity to provide the service.
Meal photos may be analyzed by OpenAI Vision when you use AI logging. We cache results to reduce API costs.
You can export or delete your data anytime in Settings. Deletion is permanent.
We do not sell personal data. Team captains see aggregate participation stats, not private photos set to hidden.
termsSections: NutriCrew provides estimated nutrition information — not medical advice. Consult professionals for health decisions.
Fair play: duplicate photos and non-food images may be rejected. Cheating reduces points.
Telegram Stars purchases are processed by Telegram. Refunds follow Telegram policies.
We may update features and pricing. Continued use means acceptance of updates.
Misuse (spam, harassment) may result in account removal.

common.loadingSlow: Waking up Render (free tier). Usually 20–50s — keep the app open…
common.apiNotConfigured: Could not reach the API. Check that Render is running.
common.apiTimeout: Server did not respond in time. Wait a minute and tap Retry.
common.apiBadResponse: Invalid server response. Check Render and Vercel deploys.
common.telegramRequired: Open the mini app from your Telegram bot button, not a browser tab.
common.telegramAuthFailed: Telegram did not pass sign-in data. Close the app and reopen from the bot.
common.telegramMenuButtonHint: Menu Button is set by the bot API. Profile «Open App» is separate: @BotFather → Bot Settings → Configure Mini App → Enable + URL https://nutricrew-miniapp.vercel.app. Then send /start to the bot.
common.invalidTelegramAuth: Auth failed. On Render, BOT_TOKEN must be the token of the same bot you opened the app from.
common.botNotConfigured: BOT_TOKEN is missing on Render. Add your @BotFather token and redeploy the service.

registration.title: Welcome, {{name}}!
registration.subtitle: Start logging meals right away. Profile is needed after a few logs — for personalized tips and team points.
registration.gateSubtitle: You've logged meals — add weight, height and age to continue and unlock your team.
registration.note: Used for calorie estimates and personalized nutrition tips. You can update later in the wellness guide.
registration.invalidWeight: Enter a valid weight between 30 and 300 kg.
registration.invalidHeight: Enter a valid height between 120 and 230 cm.
registration.invalidAge: Enter a valid age between 14 and 100 years.

guest.modeHint: Log meals without a profile. {{left}} logs left before registration.
guest.teamBoostHint: Team members earn boosted points — join a team to keep the bonus.

postRegistration.title: Great, {{name}}!
postRegistration.subtitle: Based on your BMI and age, we suggest a diet and exercise plan to start with.

reason.underweight: Higher protein and strength work support healthy weight gain (age {{age}}).
reason.normal: Balanced eating and mixed training fit a healthy BMI at age {{age}}.
reason.overweight: DASH-style eating and regular cardio help cardiometabolic health (age {{age}}).
reason.obese: Lower-calorie, fiber-forward patterns plus daily movement are recommended (age {{age}}).

wellness.title: Wellness guide
wellness.subtitle: Evidence-based diets, body-type nutrition & exercise plans aligned with WHO, USDA, NIH & ACSM guidelines.
wellness.disclaimer: General educational information only — not medical advice. Consult a physician or registered dietitian before changing diet or exercise, especially if you have chronic conditions.
wellness.sources: Based on: WHO Healthy Diet (2024), USDA Dietary Guidelines, NIH DASH, EAT-Lancet Commission, ACSM Physical Activity Guidelines.
wellness.mealPlanHint: Sample 7-day menu (~1,500–1,800 kcal/day). Dishes refresh each calendar week (UTC). Adjust portions to your calorie target.

muscleGroupGuide.chest: Chest drives pressing patterns (flat and incline). Lead with compounds, then isolation. Rest 2–3 min on heavy sets.
muscleGroupGuide.back: Back: vertical pulls for width, horizontal rows for thickness. Retract shoulder blades; control the negative.
muscleGroupGuide.shoulders: Shoulders have front, side, and rear delts. Press for strength, raises for volume, face pulls for joint balance.
muscleGroupGuide.biceps: Biceps: full range, elbows fixed. Two to three exercises per session is enough; avoid cheating heavy curls.
muscleGroupGuide.triceps: Triceps make up most arm size. Combine pressing (long head) with extensions (lateral/medial heads).
muscleGroupGuide.core: Core is anti-extension and anti-rotation, not just crunches. Breathe; quality beats rep count.
muscleGroupGuide.quads: Quads: squats and knee extension. Knees track toes; depth depends on mobility without rounding the back.
muscleGroupGuide.hamstrings: Hamstrings: hip hinge and knee flexion. Drive hips forward at the top of each rep.
muscleGroupGuide.glutes: Glutes: bridges, hinges, and lunges. Pause and squeeze at lockout without hyperextending the spine.
muscleGroupGuide.calves: Calves: standing hits gastrocnemius, seated hits soleus. Full ROM with a pause at stretch and contraction.

ectomorph.name: Ectomorph
ectomorph.summary: Lean build, fast metabolism, gains muscle slowly.
traits: Needs calorie surplus to build muscle
ectomorph.nutrition: Prioritize nutrient-dense calories: whole grains, starchy vegetables, healthy fats (olive oil, nuts, avocado). Protein 1.4–1.8 g/kg/day (ISSN) spread across meals. Avoid excessive fasting.
ectomorph.activity: Emphasize progressive resistance training 3–4×/week. Keep cardio moderate (ACSM: 150 min/week) to avoid excess calorie burn.

mesomorph.name: Mesomorph
mesomorph.summary: Athletic frame, responds quickly to training and diet.
mesomorph.nutrition: Balanced plate per USDA MyPlate: half vegetables/fruits, quarter lean protein, quarter whole grains. Protein 1.2–1.6 g/kg/day. Adjust calories to goal (maintain, cut, or bulk).
mesomorph.activity: Combine strength (2–4×/week) and cardio (150–300 min/week moderate per WHO). Sport-specific drills work well.

endomorph.name: Endomorph
endomorph.summary: Stores fat more readily; benefits from protein-rich, fiber-forward eating.
endomorph.nutrition: Higher protein (1.6–2.0 g/kg/day), high fiber (25–38 g/day per USDA), low-glycemic carbs. Mediterranean or DASH patterns support cardiometabolic health (WHO/AHA).
endomorph.activity: Prioritize daily movement (NEAT) plus 150–300 min/week aerobic activity (WHO). Add resistance 2–3×/week to preserve lean mass.

mediterranean.name: Mediterranean diet
mediterranean.summary: Plant-rich pattern with olive oil, fish, and moderate wine — top-rated for heart health.
mediterranean.description: Emphasizes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, legumes, nuts, olive oil as main fat, fish 2×/week, limited red meat and processed foods. Aligns with WHO recommendations to reduce saturated fat and increase fiber.
mediterranean.evidence: PREDIMED trial and meta-analyses show reduced cardiovascular events and improved glycemic control. Endorsed by American Heart Association.

dash.name: DASH diet
dash.summary: Low sodium, high potassium pattern designed to lower blood pressure.
dash.source: NIH National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute
dash.description: DASH (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) limits sodium to 1,500–2,300 mg/day, increases potassium, magnesium, and calcium through whole foods. Ideal for endomorph and mesomorph types managing weight or blood pressure.
dash.evidence: NIH-funded trials demonstrate systolic BP reduction of 8–14 mmHg. Recommended by US Dietary Guidelines for hypertension.

plantForward.name: Plant-forward (Flexitarian)
plantForward.summary: Mostly plants with optional small amounts of animal protein — EAT-Lancet aligned.
plantForward.description: Half the plate from vegetables and fruits, whole grains and legumes as staples, animal products as garnish. Supports sustainability and reduces saturated fat per WHO healthy diet targets.
plantForward.evidence: EAT-Lancet planetary health diet links plant-rich patterns to lower CVD mortality and reduced environmental footprint.

highProtein.name: High-protein balanced
highProtein.summary: Elevated protein for active adults preserving muscle during fat loss or lean gain.
highProtein.description: Protein 1.6–2.2 g/kg/day distributed over 4 meals (0.25–0.4 g/kg each). Remaining calories from complex carbs and unsaturated fats. Suited to ectomorph and mesomorph training phases.
foods: Whey or plant protein if needed
highProtein.evidence: ISSN meta-analyses show improved lean mass retention during caloric deficit when protein ≥1.6 g/kg/day combined with resistance training.

balancedPlate.name: USDA MyPlate balanced
balancedPlate.summary: Simple portion model: half produce, quarter protein, quarter grains.
balancedPlate.description: Official US guidance for healthy eating across life stages. Focus on variety, nutrient density, and limiting added sugars, saturated fat, and sodium per Dietary Guidelines 2020–2025.
balancedPlate.evidence: Foundation of US public health nutrition policy; aligns with WHO limits on free sugars (<10% energy) and salt (<5 g/day).

nutriRun.tagline: A tiny spirit sprints through a macro world — jump, glide, wall-run, and punch!
nutriRun.menuHint: Auto-run platformer with 6 worlds. Collect glow motes for stars.
nutriRun.mapHint: Stages in {{world}} — reach the score goal, collect motes for up to 3 stars.
nutriRun.bonusLocked: Beat 5 bosses to unlock Star Glade ({{count}}/{{need}}).
nutriRun.controls: Tap: jump · air tap: glide · wall tap: wall-jump · tap near enemies: punch
nutriRun.gameOverHint: Score {{score}}. Tap to try endless mode.

game.title: NutriBird
game.subtitle: NutriBird mini-game — coming back soon.
game.comingSoonBody: The mini-game is temporarily unavailable. Team quests and bonuses will return when it's back.
game.tapHint: Tap to jump. In the air — glide. On walls — wall-jump. Near junk food — attack.
game.worldMapHint: Pick a stage in {{world}}. Reach the score goal to unlock the next.
game.worldLocked: Complete the previous world to unlock.
game.legendRunner: 🏃 Auto-runner — jump gaps and platforms. Quick run is endless; world map has 5 worlds × 9 stages.
game.legendGlide: 🪶 Tap in the air to glide with a slow fall.
game.legendWallRun: 🧱 Run along vertical walls — tap to wall-jump off.
game.legendAttack: 👊 Tap near junk food on the ground to punch it away.
game.legendWorldMap: 🗺 Beat stage score targets for up to 3 stars and unlock new worlds.
game.startHint: NutriBird runs on its own! Quick run for high scores, or open the world map for crafted stages.
game.legendTrees: 🌲 Trees below, junk food above — the gap narrows as you level up.
game.legendFruit: 🍎🍑🍇 Fruit slims the bird and grants ghost — pass through obstacles. Nutrition slowly decays without fruit.
game.legendSpeed: 🔥 Orange nitro stacks to x5 ORANGE NITRO (max 10 seconds total). Each orange adds a stack!
game.legendJunk: 🍬🍳🍔🍕🥤 Candy, egg & bacon, spinning burger, pizza and soda above — don't hit them! At min size, eat 🍔 or 🍕 once.
game.legendSkyFood: 🌭 Sausages, links and pasta drift in the sky — just for fun.
game.legendMountain: ⛰ Low mountains rise from the water with snowy peaks — fly above! A tiny climber scales the slope.
game.legendElevations: ⛰️ Each run cycles valley, climb, and dungeon — backdrop follows the biome.
game.legendNight: 🌅 At sunset the sun dips behind the mountains; stars and moon at night (~1 min cycle).
game.legendWolves: 🐺 At night, small wolves on the hills howl at the moon.
game.legendMeteor: ☄️ Meteors fall day and night — they leave craters on the ground!
game.legendPtero: 🦅 From level 7 a pterodactyl flies overhead — harmless!
game.legendBoss: 🐗 Level 100 boss! Two nitro oranges + slow-mo to admire it. Defeat for x7 boss energy boost at min size.
game.musicHint: Tap the game to enable sound. Check device volume.
game.leaderboardEmpty: No scores yet — be the first!
game.legendCombo: 🔥 Combo ×5 every 5 trees in a row — bonus points. Close brush past a tree: +1.
game.mealShieldActive: Meal logged — 1 shield hit this run!
game.upgradeError: Not enough XP or max level.
game.seasonSummer: ☀️ Summer vitality — more nutrition from fruit
game.legendShield: 🛡 Log a meal — 1 shield hit per run (blocks one crash).
game.legendDuel: 👻 Purple ghost — async duel opponent replay.

quiz.title: Calorie Quiz
quiz.subtitle: 186 products · kcal per 100 g/ml via Atwater (WHO/FAO). No alcoholic drinks.
quiz.question: How many kilocalories (kcal)?
quiz.startHint: {{count}} unique questions per round. Next round uses different products. Repeats only after 50+ products seen overall.
quiz.gameOverHint: You scored {{score}} / {{total}}. A new set of products awaits.
quiz.footnote: {{count}} questions per round · kcal via Atwater (WHO/FAO).
quiz.macrosLine: P {{protein}} g · F {{fat}} g · C {{carbs}} g per 100 g/ml

calculator.formHint: Compare 5 BMR formulas + activity factor (WHO/ACSM). Pick a primary formula for your target and macros.
calculator.macrosHint: Protein based on ISSN guidelines (g/kg body weight); remainder split carbs/fats.
calculator.safeMinimumWarning: Target raised to {{min}} kcal/day — deficits below this are not recommended without medical supervision.
calculator.primaryFormulaHint: Used for your calorie target and macros. All formulas are compared below.
calculator.compareHint: BMR = at rest; TDEE = with activity; target = adjusted for your goal (±250…750 kcal).
calculator.formulaNote: Formulas: Mifflin–St Jeor (1990), Harris–Benedict revised (1984), Harris–Benedict original (1919), Owen (1986), Schofield/WHO (1985). Activity: 1.2–1.9. 7700 kcal ≈ 1 kg body mass.
calculator.disclaimer: Estimates only. Needs vary with muscle mass, hormones, and health. Mifflin–St Jeor is often most accurate for modern populations.
